name: Vapi
description: Vapi is a developer-first voice AI platform for building, testing, and deploying conversational voice agents. It handles real-time audio infrastructure, STT, LLM orchestration, and TTS with 200+ model integrations, sub-500ms latency, and enterprise-grade reliability. Powers 300M+ calls across 500K+ developers.
oneLiner: The most configurable API to build voice AI products and scale phone operations.
funding: $20M Series A (Dec 2024) led by Bessemer Venture Partners; YC-backed; ~$130M valuation
pricing: Pay-as-you-go: $0.05/min + model costs at-cost; $10 free credit; Enterprise with volume pricing

screenshots.alt: Vapi Dashboard — voice agent management and configuration interface

uxAnalysis.builderFlow: Dual-track: developers use REST API (4,200+ config points) with SDKs and CLI. Product teams use Dashboard UI. Create agent by defining instructions/tools, selecting models (200+ STT/LLM/TTS options), integrating data via tools, then testing with built-in calling and evaluation. Squads for multi-agent. 1000+ templates for rapid prototyping.
strengths: Automated testing with simulated voice agents
weaknesses: Visual builder deprecated (Workflows to Squads transition)
Platform cost stacks on top of model costs
Young company with limited enterprise case studies
notableFeatures: Automated voice testing with hallucination detection
Custom SIP and warm transfers for enterprise telephony

recentDevelopments: Dec 2024: $20M Series A led by Bessemer at ~$130M valuation

subCriteria.name: Agent Builder UX
subCriteria.rationale: API-native with 4,200+ config points. SDKs, CLI, MCP support. Exhaustive docs.

subCriteria.name: Time-to-Value
subCriteria.rationale: Dashboard UI available. Workflows deprecated in favor of Squads. Composer for quick creation.

subCriteria.name: Debugging & Testing
subCriteria.rationale: 1000+ pre-made templates. Quickstarts for phone and web calls.

subCriteria.name: Documentation & Learning
subCriteria.rationale: Automated testing with simulated agents. A/B experiments. Evaluation scorecards.

subCriteria.name: Multi-Agent Coordination
subCriteria.rationale: Squads enable multi-agent with handoff between specialized agents.

subCriteria.name: Human-in-the-Loop Design
subCriteria.rationale: Transfer call nodes for human escalation. Warm transfers. Limited structured approval.

subCriteria.name: Workflow Templates
subCriteria.rationale: API-first, single-developer oriented. Limited multi-user dashboard features.

subCriteria.name: Team Development
subCriteria.rationale: API-first, single-developer oriented. Limited multi-user dashboard features.

subCriteria.name: Native Connectors
subCriteria.rationale: 200+ integrations (STT, LLM, TTS) with BYOM support.

subCriteria.name: API & Protocol Standards
subCriteria.rationale: Native Twilio, custom SIP, Vapi numbers, SMS, web widget.

subCriteria.name: Authentication & Access
subCriteria.rationale: 40+ app integrations. Relies on tool calling and Make/n8n/Zapier.

subCriteria.name: Data Pipeline & RAG
subCriteria.rationale: MCP support, CLI, GitHub integration, webhooks, OpenAI-compatible API.

subCriteria.name: Guardrails & Kill Switches
subCriteria.rationale: SOC2, HIPAA, PCI covering healthcare, finance, payments.

subCriteria.name: Audit Trail & Compliance
subCriteria.rationale: SSO/RBAC on enterprise tier. Basic API key management on free tier.

subCriteria.name: RBAC & Access Control
subCriteria.rationale: Built-in AI guardrails per assistant. Configurable content safety.

subCriteria.name: Explainability & Transparency
subCriteria.rationale: 14-day call history (free), custom retention (enterprise). Limited audit trail docs.

subCriteria.name: Deployment Flexibility
subCriteria.rationale: Scale to millions of calls. 300M+ processed. Elastic infrastructure.

subCriteria.name: Scaling & Monitoring
subCriteria.rationale: Advanced analytics, call insights, structured outputs. Observability stack support.

subCriteria.name: Cost Management
subCriteria.rationale: Usage-based but $0.05/min platform + model costs stack up. Competitor criticism on hidden costs.

subCriteria.name: Ecosystem Maturity
subCriteria.rationale: 500K+ developers. 250K+ community. 13K+ support topics. Strong for 2023 startup.

scoringRationale: Vapi scores 72/100. Builder Experience (14) world-class API but visual builder in flux. Deployment (14) massive scale but cloud-only. Integration (14) deep models/telephony, sparse business tools. Governance (12) strong compliance, basic RBAC. Collaboration (10) and Ecosystem (8) weakest — minimal team features, young company.

communityBuzz: Voice agents are booming — r/AIVoice_Agents actively discussing Vapi and competitors
Platform cost (/bin/zsh.05/min) on top of model costs is a frequent pricing complaint
Squads replacing deprecated Workflows — transition causing some user friction
SOC2/HIPAA/PCI compliance praised for enabling regulated industry deployments

walkthrough.title: Building a Voice Customer Support Agent with Vapi

steps.title: Create Your Voice Assistant
steps.description: Define your assistant in Vapi's dashboard or via API: select a voice model, set the system prompt with your support guidelines, and configure the conversation personality. Choose from 100+ voice options across multiple languages.

steps.title: Connect Telephony Infrastructure
steps.description: Assign a phone number via Twilio, Vonage, or Vapi's built-in numbers. Configure inbound call routing rules, set business hours, and define overflow behavior. Sub-500ms latency ensures natural, real-time conversation.

steps.title: Add Tools and Knowledge Base
steps.description: Give your agent capabilities: connect your CRM to pull customer records, link a knowledge base for product FAQs, add a calendar tool for appointment booking. Vapi's function calling lets the agent take actions mid-conversation.

steps.title: Test, Deploy, and Scale
steps.description: Test with live calls in the dashboard. Monitor real-time transcripts, latency metrics, and tool call success rates. Scale to thousands of concurrent calls with Vapi's infrastructure — the same platform powering 300M+ calls across 500K+ developers.
